response:
Emily divides the 24 residents into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups. Each group receives 3 initial writing prompts, so 4 groups × 3 prompts = 12 prompts. She also prepares an additional set of 10 prompts to be shared equally among the groups, which means each group gets 10 ÷ 4 = 2.5 prompts. Since prompts cannot be split, Emily will need to round up to ensure each group gets at least 3 prompts from the extra set. This means she needs to prepare 4 × 3 = 12 extra prompts. 

In total, Emily needs 12 (initial) + 12 (extra) = 24 prompts for the session. The library of prompts must be carefully curated to ensure a wide variety of topics that resonate with the participants. The library also plays a crucial role in maintaining a steady supply of creative ideas for future sessions. The END.
